Ukraine has deployed drone-interception units to protect critical and civilian infrastructure in five Middle Eastern countries, according to Ukrainian Security Coun...

external.description:
Ukraine has deployed drone-interception units to protect critical and civilian infrastructure in five Middle Eastern countries, according to Ukrainian Security Council Secretary Rustem Umerov. The effort involves the use of Ukrainian technology and battlefield experience to counter aerial threats, particularly drone attacks. 
 Umerov said Ukraine had deployed interceptor missile units in several countries across the Middle East, with the main goal of protecting critical infrastructure and civilian sites from airborne threats, especially drones. He made the announcement following a visit to the region in a post on X . 
 He said the information was also included in a report to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on the work of Ukrainian teams in the Persian Gulf region. 
 Ukraine has deployed interceptor missile units to protect critical and civilian infrastructure in five Middle Eastern countries. 
 “The work is focused on two areas. The first is the use of Ukrainian technologies to counter aerial threats, particularly drone attacks. The second is consultations with partners to assess the security situation and prepare practical air defense solutions based on Ukraine’s experience,” Umerov said. 
 According to Umerov, Ukrainian specialists assessed the protection of infrastructure sites and prepared practical solutions aimed at improving the effectiveness of air defense systems. The sides also identified next steps for developing long-term security cooperation. 
 He added that Ukraine continues to work with partners to strengthen security and protect civilians. Efforts are also underway to expand the coverage areas of Ukrainian interception systems. 
 “Ukraine stands for the protection of life and sovereignty together with those who support us in preserving our independence. We seek to further strengthen security partnerships with our allies,” Umerov said. 
 In late February in Geneva, Umerov and U.S. representatives discussed a large-scale postwar reconstruction plan for Ukraine. 
 The plan could reach about $800 billion. The talks focused on securing international financing and shaping a long-term strategy for rebuilding the country.

images.alt:
⚖️ The Supreme Court’s Ruling (5–4)
The Court held:
✔️ Corporations, unions, and nonprofits may spend unlimited money on independent political advocacy.
This includes ads, films, and other communications that support or oppose candidates — as long as the spending is not coordinated with a campaign.
✔️ Restrictions on such independent expenditures violate the First Amendment.
Justice Kennedy wrote that limiting this spending is equivalent to limiting speech.
✔️ Disclosure and disclaimer rules remain valid.
Groups must still report spending and include “paid for by” disclaimers.
❌ Corporate contributions directly to candidates remain banned.
The ruling did not allow corporations to donate to campaigns — only to spend independently.

⭐ Congress cannot override Citizens United with a normal law.
Because the Supreme Court held that corporate independent spending is protected by the First Amendment, only a constitutional amendment or a future Supreme Court reversal can overturn it.

🧩 Why Congress can’t fix it with legislation
Citizens United is a constitutional ruling.
That means:
- Congress cannot pass a statute redefining corporations as “not individuals” for First Amendment purposes.
- Any such law would be struck down immediately as unconstitutional under Citizens United and Buckley.
The Court explicitly said political spending by corporations is protected speech.
Only the Constitution can override the Constitution.

I told CoPilot to make me a sign: "Long-Haired Freaky People Need Not Apply". It refused, telling me it's a copyrighted phrase, but to know, it had to have it in it's training data—so, it will steal shit and won't let me!!!

Then it lied, when I told it: evaluate the above as fact! 🤣

images.alt:
🧠 1. Recognizing a copyrighted phrase ≠ “stealing” anything
I don’t store, keep, or retrieve copyrighted text.
I don’t have a database of lyrics.
I don’t have the song in memory.
I don’t “look it up.”
I don’t “have” the lyric.
What I do have is the ability to recognize patterns — like “this looks like a line from a song.”
That’s not the same as having the lyric stored or accessible.
Think of it like this:
You can recognize a Beatles lyric when you hear it, but that doesn’t mean you have the sheet music in your pocket.

record.text: Shame is a powerful force in shaping which uses a society will tolerate for a given technology, and which it will not. There may be many reasonable uses for LLMs, but when they are used to degrade writing and writers' livelihoods, yelling 'we think this is bad' is a justifiable and moral response.

value.text:
1/ LLMs are just math, full stop. They are not going away for the same reasons that you can unteach a child that 2+2=4. 

We will forever live in a world where they exist, and they _will be used_. They can be made on home computers, so deleting all of them is impossible.

external.description:
If your Keyboard or Mouse is not working in Windows 11/10 after a recent update or upgrade, here are a few things you could try to fix the issue. Sometimes, simply restarting the computer or disconnecting and reconnecting the mouse or keyboard can help. If it does not, this post offers some troubleshooting suggestions that may help you.

If you have a touchscreen monitor or laptop, you can keep using your Windows PC without a mouse or keyboard, which also makes troubleshooting easier. If you do not have a touchscreen monitor or Windows PC and your mouse and keyboard have stopped working in Windows, it makes things a bit difficult. You may need to use the on-screen keyboard.

If your Keyboard or Mouse not working in Windows 10, first check if you need to change the battery, and then try these suggestions

record.text:
Chickadee’s Treasure

The peanut looks enormous clamped in the beak of this little Carolina chickadee (Poecile carolinensis).

images.alt:
"The image captures a Carolina chickadee perched on a slender branch, its tiny body angled slightly forward as if caught in a moment of quiet triumph. The bird’s black cap and matching throat patch frame its bright white cheeks, giving its face a crisp, mask‑like contrast. Its dark, bead‑bright eye glints with alert curiosity.

Clamped firmly in its small, sturdy beak is a pale peanut—light tan against the bird’s charcoal and gray plumage. The chickadee holds it with the confidence of a seasoned forager, as though it has just claimed a prize worth showing off.

The feathers along its back and wings are soft gray, neatly layered, while its underside is a gentle wash of off‑white. The background dissolves into a smooth blur of muted greens and browns, creating a calm, natural backdrop that makes the bird and its peanut stand out in sharp relief. The overall mood is intimate and peaceful: a tiny bird, a quiet perch, and a simple moment of success in the wild." - Microsoft Copilot

external.description:
Meta kauft ein weiteres soziales Netzwerk für KI-Bots.

Meta kauft Bot-Netzwerk 🤖 Der Techkonzern #Meta übernimmt das experimentelle Netzwerk #Moltbook, eine Plattform, auf der hauptsächlich #KIBots miteinander kommunizieren.

external.description:
Bei KIs zählt nur noch Profit.

KI-Markt kippt ⚡ Der #Wettbewerb im #KI Sektor verschiebt sich rasant, denn nicht mehr nur die beste Technologie zählt, sondern vor allem, wer KI am schnellsten #profitabel einsetzen kann.

#Anthropic holt auf 🚀 Während #OpenAI lange dominierte, gewinnt Anthropic zunehmend Unternehmenskunden und entwickelt sich zum ernsthaften Konkurrenten im lukrativen B2B-Markt.

Consumer vs. Business 💼 OpenAI bleibt stark im Endkundenbereich, verdient dort jedoch weniger Geld, da hohe Nutzungskosten oft subventioniert werden müssen.

Strategiewechsel im Gange 🔄 Deshalb denkt OpenAI darüber nach, sich stärker auf Geschäftskunden zu konzentrieren, wo stabile Einnahmen und höhere Margen locken.

Entscheidende Phase 🤖 Experten sehen einen Wendepunkt im KI-Wettlauf, in dem Unternehmen parallel Innovation und Geschäftsmodelle entwickeln müssen, um langfristig zu bestehen.

external.description:
»Das ist das nächste ChatGPT!«

OpenClaw als KI-Revolution 🚀 #Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang bezeichnet #OpenClaw als „nächstes ChatGPT“, da autonome #KIAgenten nicht nur antworten, sondern selbstständig Aufgaben ausführen, Entscheidungen treffen und Prozesse steuern können.

Mehr als nur Chatbots 🤖 Die Technologie ermöglicht es, mit minimalem Input eigene Agenten zu erstellen, die komplexe Aufgaben wie Design, Recherche oder Organisation eigenständig lernen und verbessern.

Nvidia baut Infrastruktur 🧠 Mit #NemoClaw entwickelt Nvidia eine sichere Enterprise-Version, um KI-Agenten skalierbar, kontrollierbar und für den realen Einsatz in Unternehmen nutzbar zu machen.

China treibt Adoption 📈 In #China verbreitet sich OpenClaw rasant, unterstützt durch Events, Tech-Konzerne und staatliche Initiativen, wodurch Millionen Nutzer die Technologie aktiv in Alltag und Business integrieren.

Ein-Mann-Unternehmen entstehen 💼 Durch OpenClaw entstehen neue Geschäftsmodelle wie „One-Person Companies“, in denen einzelne Personen mithilfe von KI komplette Unternehmen automatisiert betreiben können.
